easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, any_storage_paths: Optional[List[Union[str, int]]] = None, exclude_storage_paths: Optional[List[Union[str, int]]] = None, owner: int | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, exclude_owners: Optional[List[int]] = None, custom_fields: Optional[List[Union[str, int]]] = None, any_custom_fields: Optional[List[Union[str, int]]] = None, exclude_custom_fields: Optional[List[Union[str, int]]] = None, custom_field_query: Optional[List[Any]] = None, archive_serial_number: int | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, archive_serial_number_from: int | None = None, archive_serial_number_till: int | None = None, created_after: datetime.date | str | None = None, created_before: datetime.date | str | None = None, added_after: datetime.date | datetime.datetime | str | None = None, added_from: datetime.date | datetime.datetime | str | None = None, added_before: datetime.date | datetime.datetime | str | None = None, added_until: datetime.date | datetime.datetime | str | None = None, modified_after: datetime.date | datetime.datetime | str | None = None, modified_from: datetime.date | datetime.datetime | str | None = None, modified_before: datetime.date | datetime.datetime | str | None = None, modified_until: datetime.date | datetime.datetime | str | None = None, checksum: str | None = None, page_size: int = 25, page: int | None = None, ordering: str | None = None, descending: bool = False, max_results: int | None = None, on_page: Callable[[int, int | None], None] | None = None) -> easypaperless.PagedResult[Document]: 
245    async def list(
246        self,
247        *,
248        search: str | None = None,
249        search_mode: str = "title_or_content",
250        ids: List[int] | None = None,
251        tags: List[int | str] | None = None,
252        any_tags: List[int | str] | None = None,
253        exclude_tags: List[int | str] | None = None,
254        correspondent: int | str | None | Unset = UNSET,
255        any_correspondent: List[int | str] | None = None,
256        exclude_correspondents: List[int | str] | None = None,
257        document_type: int | str | None | Unset = UNSET,
258        document_type_name_contains: str | None = None,
259        document_type_name_exact: str | None = None,
260        any_document_type: List[int | str] | None = None,
261        exclude_document_types: List[int | str] | None = None,
262        storage_path: int | str | None | Unset = UNSET,
263        any_storage_paths: List[int | str] | None = None,
264        exclude_storage_paths: List[int | str] | None = None,
265        owner: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
266        exclude_owners: List[int] | None = None,
267        custom_fields: List[int | str] | None = None,
268        any_custom_fields: List[int | str] | None = None,
269        exclude_custom_fields: List[int | str] | None = None,
270        custom_field_query: List[Any] | None = None,
271        archive_serial_number: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
272        archive_serial_number_from: int | None = None,
273        archive_serial_number_till: int | None = None,
274        created_after: date | str | None = None,
275        created_before: date | str | None = None,
276        added_after: date | datetime | str | None = None,
277        added_from: date | datetime | str | None = None,
278        added_before: date | datetime | str | None = None,
279        added_until: date | datetime | str | None = None,
280        modified_after: date | datetime | str | None = None,
281        modified_from: date | datetime | str | None = None,
282        modified_before: date | datetime | str | None = None,
283        modified_until: date | datetime | str | None = None,
284        checksum: str | None = None,
285        page_size: int = 25,
286        page: int | None = None,
287        ordering: str | None = None,
288        descending: bool = False,
289        max_results: int | None = None,
290        on_page: Callable[[int, int | None], None] | None = None,
291    ) -> PagedResult[Document]:
292        """Return a filtered list of documents.
293
294        All tag, correspondent, document-type, storage-path, and custom-field
295        parameters accept either integer IDs or string names.
296
297        When ``page`` is ``None`` (the default), all pages are fetched
298        automatically and ``next`` / ``previous`` in the returned
299        :class:`~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult` are always
300        ``None`` — even if ``max_results`` truncates the final result set.
301        ``count`` always reflects the server total, not the truncated length.
302        When ``page`` is set to a specific integer, only that one page is
303        fetched and ``next`` / ``previous`` contain the raw API values.
304
305        Args:
306            search: Search string.  Behaviour depends on ``search_mode``.
307            search_mode: How ``search`` is applied.  One of:
308                ``"title_or_content"`` *(default)*, ``"title"``, ``"query"``,
309                ``"original_filename"``.
310            ids: Return only documents whose ID is in this list.
311            tags: Documents must have **all** of these tags (AND semantics).
312            any_tags: Documents must have **at least one** of these tags.
313            exclude_tags: Documents must have **none** of these tags.
314            correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to this correspondent.
315                Pass ``None`` to return only documents with no correspondent set.
316            any_correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to any of these.
317            exclude_correspondents: Exclude documents assigned to any of these.
318            document_type: Filter to documents of exactly this type.
319                Pass ``None`` to return only documents with no document type set.
320            document_type_name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on document type name.
321            document_type_name_exact: Case-insensitive exact match on document type name.
322            any_document_type: Filter to documents whose type is any of these.
323            exclude_document_types: Exclude documents whose type is any of these.
324            storage_path: Filter to documents assigned to this storage path.
325                Pass ``None`` to return only documents with no storage path set.
326            any_storage_paths: Filter to documents assigned to any of these paths.
327            exclude_storage_paths: Exclude documents assigned to any of these paths.
328            owner: Filter to documents owned by this user ID.
329                Pass ``None`` to return only documents with no owner set.
330            exclude_owners: Exclude documents owned by any of these user IDs.
331            custom_fields: Documents must have **all** of these custom fields set.
332            any_custom_fields: Documents must have **at least one** of these fields.
333            exclude_custom_fields: Documents must have **none** of these fields.
334            custom_field_query: Filter documents by custom field values using a nested
335                query structure. See the `paperless-ngx API docs
336                <https://docs.paperless-ngx.com/api/#filtering-by-custom-fields>`_ for
337                the query format.
338            archive_serial_number: Filter by exact archive serial number.
339                Pass ``None`` to return only documents with no ASN set.
340            archive_serial_number_from: Filter by ASN >= this value.
341            archive_serial_number_till: Filter by ASN <= this value.
342            created_after: Only documents created after this date.
343                String input must be ISO-8601: ``"YYYY-MM-DD"``.
344            created_before: Only documents created before this date.
345                String input must be ISO-8601: ``"YYYY-MM-DD"``.
346            added_after: Only documents added after this date/time.
347                String input must be ISO-8601: ``"YYYY-MM-DD"`` for date precision or
348                ``"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS"`` for datetime precision.
349            added_from: Only documents added on or after this date/time.
350                String input must be ISO-8601: ``"YYYY-MM-DD"`` or ``"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS"``.
351            added_before: Only documents added before this date/time.
352                String input must be ISO-8601: ``"YYYY-MM-DD"`` or ``"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS"``.
353            added_until: Only documents added on or before this date/time.
354                String input must be ISO-8601: ``"YYYY-MM-DD"`` or ``"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS"``.
355            modified_after: Only documents modified after this date/time.
356                String input must be ISO-8601: ``"YYYY-MM-DD"`` or ``"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS"``.
357            modified_from: Only documents modified on or after this date/time.
358                String input must be ISO-8601: ``"YYYY-MM-DD"`` or ``"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS"``.
359            modified_before: Only documents modified before this date/time.
360                String input must be ISO-8601: ``"YYYY-MM-DD"`` or ``"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS"``.
361            modified_until: Only documents modified on or before this date/time.
362                String input must be ISO-8601: ``"YYYY-MM-DD"`` or ``"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS"``.
363            checksum: MD5 checksum of the original file (exact match).
364            page_size: Number of results per API page.  Default: ``25``.
365            page: Return only this specific page (1-based).
366            ordering: Field name to sort by.
367            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction.
368            max_results: Stop after collecting this many documents.
369            on_page: Callback invoked after each page fetch.
370
371        Returns:
372            :class:`~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult` of
373            :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document` objects.
374        """
375        logger.info("Listing documents")
376        resolver = self._core._resolver
377        params: dict[str, Any] = {"page_size": page_size}
378
379        if search is not None:
380            api_param = _SEARCH_MODE_MAP.get(search_mode, "search")
381            params[api_param] = search
382
383        if ids is not None:
384            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
385
386        if tags is not None:
387            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
388            params["tags__id__all"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
389
390        if any_tags is not None:
391            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("tags", any_tags)
392            params["tags__id__in"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
393
394        if exclude_tags is not None:
395            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("tags", exclude_tags)
396            params["tags__id__none"] = ",".join(str(t) for t in resolved)
397
398        if any_correspondent is not None:
399            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("correspondents", any_correspondent)
400            params["correspondent__id__in"] = ",".join(str(c) for c in resolved)
401        elif not isinstance(correspondent, Unset):
402            if correspondent is None:
403                params["correspondent__isnull"] = "true"
404            else:
405                resolved_id = await resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
406                params["correspondent__id__in"] = resolved_id
407
408        if exclude_correspondents is not None:
409            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("correspondents", exclude_correspondents)
410            params["correspondent__id__none"] = ",".join(str(c) for c in resolved)
411
412        if document_type_name_contains is not None:
413            params["document_type__name__icontains"] = document_type_name_contains
414        if document_type_name_exact is not None:
415            params["document_type__name__iexact"] = document_type_name_exact
416
417        if any_document_type is not None:
418            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("document_types", any_document_type)
419            params["document_type__id__in"] = ",".join(str(d) for d in resolved)
420        elif not isinstance(document_type, Unset):
421            if document_type is None:
422                params["document_type__isnull"] = "true"
423            else:
424                resolved_id = await resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
425                params["document_type"] = resolved_id
426
427        if exclude_document_types is not None:
428            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("document_types", exclude_document_types)
429            params["document_type__id__none"] = ",".join(str(d) for d in resolved)
430
431        if any_storage_paths is not None:
432            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("storage_paths", any_storage_paths)
433            params["storage_path__id__in"] = ",".join(str(s) for s in resolved)
434        elif not isinstance(storage_path, Unset):
435            if storage_path is None:
436                params["storage_path__isnull"] = "true"
437            else:
438                resolved_id = await resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
439                params["storage_path__id__in"] = resolved_id
440
441        if exclude_storage_paths is not None:
442            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("storage_paths", exclude_storage_paths)
443            params["storage_path__id__none"] = ",".join(str(s) for s in resolved)
444
445        if not isinstance(owner, Unset):
446            if owner is None:
447                params["owner__isnull"] = "true"
448            else:
449                params["owner__id__in"] = owner
450
451        if exclude_owners is not None:
452            params["owner__id__none"] = ",".join(str(o) for o in exclude_owners)
453
454        if custom_fields is not None:
455            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("custom_fields", custom_fields)
456            params["custom_fields__id__all"] = ",".join(str(f) for f in resolved)
457
458        if any_custom_fields is not None:
459            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("custom_fields", any_custom_fields)
460            params["custom_fields__id__in"] = ",".join(str(f) for f in resolved)
461
462        if exclude_custom_fields is not None:
463            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("custom_fields", exclude_custom_fields)
464            params["custom_fields__id__none"] = ",".join(str(f) for f in resolved)
465
466        if custom_field_query is not None:
467            params["custom_field_query"] = json.dumps(custom_field_query)
468
469        if not isinstance(archive_serial_number, Unset):
470            if archive_serial_number is None:
471                params["archive_serial_number__isnull"] = "true"
472            else:
473                params["archive_serial_number"] = archive_serial_number
474
475        if archive_serial_number_from is not None:
476            params["archive_serial_number__gte"] = archive_serial_number_from
477
478        if archive_serial_number_till is not None:
479            params["archive_serial_number__lte"] = archive_serial_number_till
480
481        if created_after is not None:
482            params["created__date__gt"] = self._format_date_value(created_after)
483
484        if created_before is not None:
485            params["created__date__lt"] = self._format_date_value(created_before)
486
487        if added_after is not None:
488            key = "added__gt" if self._is_datetime(added_after) else "added__date__gt"
489            params[key] = self._format_date_value(added_after)
490
491        if added_from is not None:
492            key = "added__gte" if self._is_datetime(added_from) else "added__date__gte"
493            params[key] = self._format_date_value(added_from)
494
495        if added_before is not None:
496            key = "added__lt" if self._is_datetime(added_before) else "added__date__lt"
497            params[key] = self._format_date_value(added_before)
498
499        if added_until is not None:
500            key = "added__lte" if self._is_datetime(added_until) else "added__date__lte"
501            params[key] = self._format_date_value(added_until)
502
503        if modified_after is not None:
504            key = "modified__gt" if self._is_datetime(modified_after) else "modified__date__gt"
505            params[key] = self._format_date_value(modified_after)
506
507        if modified_from is not None:
508            key = "modified__gte" if self._is_datetime(modified_from) else "modified__date__gte"
509            params[key] = self._format_date_value(modified_from)
510
511        if modified_before is not None:
512            key = "modified__lt" if self._is_datetime(modified_before) else "modified__date__lt"
513            params[key] = self._format_date_value(modified_before)
514
515        if modified_until is not None:
516            key = "modified__lte" if self._is_datetime(modified_until) else "modified__date__lte"
517            params[key] = self._format_date_value(modified_until)
518
519        if checksum is not None:
520            params["checksum__iexact"] = checksum
521
522        if ordering is not None:
523            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
524
525        if page is not None:
526            params["page"] = page
527            raw = await self._core._session.get_page("/documents/", params=params)
528            items = raw.items
529            if max_results is not None:
530                items = items[:max_results]
531            return PagedResult(
532                count=raw.count,
533                next=raw.next,
534                previous=raw.previous,
535                all=raw.all_ids,
536                results=[Document.model_validate(item) for item in items],
537            )
538
539        raw = await self._core._session.get_all_pages_paged(
540            "/documents/", params, max_results=max_results, on_page=on_page
541        )
542        return PagedResult(
543            count=raw.count,
544            next=raw.next,
545            previous=raw.previous,
546            all=raw.all_ids,
547            results=[Document.model_validate(item) for item in raw.items],
548        )

Return a filtered list of documents.

All tag, correspondent, document-type, storage-path, and custom-field parameters accept either integer IDs or string names.

When page is None (the default), all pages are fetched automatically and next / previous in the returned ~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult are always None — even if max_results truncates the final result set. count always reflects the server total, not the truncated length. When page is set to a specific integer, only that one page is fetched and next / previous contain the raw API values.

Arguments:
  • search: Search string. Behaviour depends on search_mode.
  • search_mode: How search is applied. One of: "title_or_content" (default), "title", "query", "original_filename".
  • ids: Return only documents whose ID is in this list.
  • tags: Documents must have all of these tags (AND semantics).
  • any_tags: Documents must have at least one of these tags.
  • exclude_tags: Documents must have none of these tags.
  • correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to this correspondent. Pass None to return only documents with no correspondent set.
  • any_correspondent: Filter to documents assigned to any of these.
  • exclude_correspondents: Exclude documents assigned to any of these.
  • document_type: Filter to documents of exactly this type. Pass None to return only documents with no document type set.
  • document_type_name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on document type name.
  • document_type_name_exact: Case-insensitive exact match on document type name.
  • any_document_type: Filter to documents whose type is any of these.
  • exclude_document_types: Exclude documents whose type is any of these.
  • storage_path: Filter to documents assigned to this storage path. Pass None to return only documents with no storage path set.
  • any_storage_paths: Filter to documents assigned to any of these paths.
  • exclude_storage_paths: Exclude documents assigned to any of these paths.
  • owner: Filter to documents owned by this user ID. Pass None to return only documents with no owner set.
  • exclude_owners: Exclude documents owned by any of these user IDs.
  • custom_fields: Documents must have all of these custom fields set.
  • any_custom_fields: Documents must have at least one of these fields.
  • exclude_custom_fields: Documents must have none of these fields.
  • custom_field_query: Filter documents by custom field values using a nested query structure. See the paperless-ngx API docs for the query format.
  • archive_serial_number: Filter by exact archive serial number. Pass None to return only documents with no ASN set.
  • archive_serial_number_from: Filter by ASN >= this value.
  • archive_serial_number_till: Filter by ASN <= this value.
  • created_after: Only documents created after this date. String input must be ISO-8601: "YYYY-MM-DD".
  • created_before: Only documents created before this date. String input must be ISO-8601: "YYYY-MM-DD".
  • added_after: Only documents added after this date/time. String input must be ISO-8601: "YYYY-MM-DD" for date precision or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS" for datetime precision.
  • added_from: Only documents added on or after this date/time. String input must be ISO-8601: "YYYY-MM-DD" or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS".
  • added_before: Only documents added before this date/time. String input must be ISO-8601: "YYYY-MM-DD" or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS".
  • added_until: Only documents added on or before this date/time. String input must be ISO-8601: "YYYY-MM-DD" or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS".
  • modified_after: Only documents modified after this date/time. String input must be ISO-8601: "YYYY-MM-DD" or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS".
  • modified_from: Only documents modified on or after this date/time. String input must be ISO-8601: "YYYY-MM-DD" or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS".
  • modified_before: Only documents modified before this date/time. String input must be ISO-8601: "YYYY-MM-DD" or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS".
  • modified_until: Only documents modified on or before this date/time. String input must be ISO-8601: "YYYY-MM-DD" or "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS".
  • checksum: MD5 checksum of the original file (exact match).
  • page_size: Number of results per API page. Default: 25.
  • page: Return only this specific page (1-based).
  • ordering: Field name to sort by.
  • descending: When True, reverses the sort direction.
  • max_results: Stop after collecting this many documents.
  • on_page: Callback invoked after each page fetch.
Returns:

~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult of ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document objects.

async def update( self, id: int, *, title: str | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, content: str | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, created: datetime.date | str | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, correspondent: int | str | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, document_type: int | str | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, storage_path: int | str | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, tags: Union[List[Union[str, int]], NoneType, easypaperless.Unset] = UNSET, archive_serial_number: int | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, custom_fields: Union[List[dict[str, Any]], NoneType, easypaperless.Unset] = UNSET, owner: int | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, set_permissions: easypaperless.SetPermissions | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, remove_inbox_tags: bool | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET) -> easypaperless.Document: 
550    async def update(
551        self,
552        id: int,
553        *,
554        title: str | Unset = UNSET,
555        content: str | Unset = UNSET,
556        created: date | str | None | Unset = UNSET,
557        correspondent: int | str | None | Unset = UNSET,
558        document_type: int | str | None | Unset = UNSET,
559        storage_path: int | str | None | Unset = UNSET,
560        tags: List[int | str] | None | Unset = UNSET,
561        archive_serial_number: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
562        custom_fields: List[dict[str, Any]] | None | Unset = UNSET,
563        owner: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
564        set_permissions: SetPermissions | None | Unset = UNSET,
565        remove_inbox_tags: bool | None | Unset = UNSET,
566    ) -> Document:
567        """Partially update a document (PATCH semantics).
568
569        Args:
570            id: Numeric ID of the document to update.
571            title: New document title.
572            content: OCR text content of the document.
573            created: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``) or a
574                :class:`~datetime.date` object.
575            correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
576                Pass ``None`` to clear the correspondent.
577            document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
578                Pass ``None`` to clear the document type.
579            storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
580                Pass ``None`` to clear the storage path.
581            tags: Full replacement list of tags (IDs or names).
582            archive_serial_number: Archive serial number to assign.
583                Pass ``None`` to clear the archive serial number.
584            custom_fields: List of ``{"field": <field_id>, "value": ...}`` dicts.
585            owner: Numeric user ID to assign as document owner.
586                Pass ``None`` to clear the owner.
587            set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets.
588                Pass ``None`` to clear all permissions (overwrite with empty).
589                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
590            remove_inbox_tags: When ``True``, removes all inbox tags from the document.
591
592        Returns:
593            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`.
594        """
595        logger.info("Updating document id=%d", id)
596        resolver = self._core._resolver
597        payload: dict[str, Any] = {}
598
599        if not isinstance(title, Unset):
600            payload["title"] = title
601        if not isinstance(content, Unset):
602            payload["content"] = content
603        if not isinstance(created, Unset):
604            payload["created"] = self._format_date_value(created) if created is not None else None
605        if not isinstance(correspondent, Unset):
606            payload["correspondent"] = (
607                None
608                if correspondent is None
609                else await resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
610            )
611        if not isinstance(document_type, Unset):
612            payload["document_type"] = (
613                None
614                if document_type is None
615                else await resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
616            )
617        if not isinstance(storage_path, Unset):
618            payload["storage_path"] = (
619                None
620                if storage_path is None
621                else await resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
622            )
623        if not isinstance(tags, Unset):
624            payload["tags"] = await resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags or [])
625        if not isinstance(archive_serial_number, Unset):
626            payload["archive_serial_number"] = archive_serial_number
627        if not isinstance(custom_fields, Unset):
628            payload["custom_fields"] = custom_fields
629        if not isinstance(owner, Unset):
630            payload["owner"] = owner
631        if not isinstance(set_permissions, Unset):
632            payload["set_permissions"] = (
633                SetPermissions().model_dump()
634                if set_permissions is None
635                else set_permissions.model_dump()
636            )
637        if not isinstance(remove_inbox_tags, Unset):
638            payload["remove_inbox_tags"] = remove_inbox_tags
639
640        resp = await self._core._session.patch(f"/documents/{id}/", json=payload)
641        return Document.model_validate(resp.json())

Partially update a document (PATCH semantics).

Arguments:
  • id: Numeric ID of the document to update.
  • title: New document title.
  • content: OCR text content of the document.
  • created: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string ("YYYY-MM-DD") or a ~datetime.date object.
  • correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name. Pass None to clear the correspondent.
  • document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name. Pass None to clear the document type.
  • storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name. Pass None to clear the storage path.
  • tags: Full replacement list of tags (IDs or names).
  • archive_serial_number: Archive serial number to assign. Pass None to clear the archive serial number.
  • custom_fields: List of {"field": <field_id>, "value": ...} dicts.
  • owner: Numeric user ID to assign as document owner. Pass None to clear the owner.
  • set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets. Pass None to clear all permissions (overwrite with empty). Omit (or pass ~easypaperless.UNSET) to leave unchanged.
  • remove_inbox_tags: When True, removes all inbox tags from the document.
Returns:

The updated ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document.

async def delete(self, id: int) -> None: 
643    async def delete(self, id: int) -> None:
644        """Permanently delete a document.
645
646        Args:
647            id: Numeric ID of the document to delete.
648
649        Raises:
650            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
651                with that ID.
652        """
653        logger.info("Deleting document id=%d", id)
654        await self._core._session.delete(f"/documents/{id}/")

Permanently delete a document.

Arguments:
  • id: Numeric ID of the document to delete.
Raises:
  • ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.
async def download(self, id: int, *, original: bool = False) -> bytes: 
656    async def download(self, id: int, *, original: bool = False) -> bytes:
657        """Download the binary content of a document.
658
659        Args:
660            id: Numeric ID of the document to download.
661            original: If ``False`` *(default)*, returns the archived PDF.
662                If ``True``, returns the original uploaded file.
663
664        Returns:
665            Raw file bytes.
666        """
667        logger.info("Downloading document id=%d (original=%s)", id, original)
668        endpoint = "download" if original else "archive"
669        resp = await self._core._session.get_download(f"/documents/{id}/{endpoint}/")
670        content_type = resp.headers.get("content-type", "")
671        if "text/html" in content_type or resp.content[:9].lower().startswith(b"<!doctype"):
672            raise ServerError(
673                f"Download returned an HTML page (content-type: {content_type!r}). "
674                "The server redirected to a login page even after re-attaching auth.",
675                status_code=None,
676            )
677        return resp.content

Download the binary content of a document.

Arguments:
  • id: Numeric ID of the document to download.
  • original: If False (default), returns the archived PDF. If True, returns the original uploaded file.
Returns:

Raw file bytes.

async def upload( self, file: str | pathlib._local.Path, *, title: str | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, created: datetime.date | str | None = None, correspondent: int | str | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, document_type: int | str | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, storage_path: int | str | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, tags: Optional[List[Union[str, int]]] = None, archive_serial_number: int | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, custom_fields: Optional[List[dict[str, Any]]] = None, wait: bool = False, poll_interval: float | None = None, poll_timeout: float | None = None) -> str | easypaperless.Document: 
679    async def upload(
680        self,
681        file: str | Path,
682        *,
683        title: str | Unset = UNSET,
684        created: date | str | None = None,
685        correspondent: int | str | None | Unset = UNSET,
686        document_type: int | str | None | Unset = UNSET,
687        storage_path: int | str | None | Unset = UNSET,
688        tags: List[int | str] | None = None,
689        archive_serial_number: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
690        custom_fields: List[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
691        wait: bool = False,
692        poll_interval: float | None = None,
693        poll_timeout: float | None = None,
694    ) -> str | Document:
695        """Upload a document to paperless-ngx.
696
697        Args:
698            file: Path to the file to upload.
699            title: Title to assign to the document.
700            created: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string (``"YYYY-MM-DD"``) or a
701                :class:`~datetime.date` object.
702            correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
703            document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
704            storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
705            tags: Tags to assign, as IDs or names.
706            archive_serial_number: Archive serial number to assign.
707            custom_fields: List of ``{"field": <field_id>, "value": ...}`` dicts.
708            wait: If ``False`` *(default)*, returns immediately with the task ID.
709                If ``True``, polls until processing completes.
710            poll_interval: Seconds between task-status checks while waiting for
711                processing to complete (requires ``wait=True``). Overrides the
712                client-level default. When omitted, falls back to the client-level
713                ``poll_interval`` (``2.0`` s unless changed at construction).
714            poll_timeout: Maximum seconds to wait before raising
715                :exc:`~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError` (requires
716                ``wait=True``). Overrides the client-level default. When omitted,
717                falls back to the client-level ``poll_timeout`` (``60.0`` s unless
718                changed at construction).
719
720        Returns:
721            The Celery task ID string when ``wait=False``, or the fully
722            processed :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.Document`
723            when ``wait=True``.
724
725        Raises:
726            ~easypaperless.exceptions.UploadError: If processing fails.
727            ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError: If timeout is exceeded.
728        """
729        resolver = self._core._resolver
730        file_path = Path(file)
731        file_bytes = file_path.read_bytes()
732        logger.info("Uploading %r (%d bytes)", file_path.name, len(file_bytes))
733
734        data: dict[str, Any] = {}
735        if not isinstance(title, Unset):
736            data["title"] = title
737        if created is not None:
738            data["created"] = self._format_date_value(created)
739        if not isinstance(correspondent, Unset) and correspondent is not None:
740            data["correspondent"] = await resolver.resolve("correspondents", correspondent)
741        if not isinstance(document_type, Unset) and document_type is not None:
742            data["document_type"] = await resolver.resolve("document_types", document_type)
743        if not isinstance(storage_path, Unset) and storage_path is not None:
744            data["storage_path"] = await resolver.resolve("storage_paths", storage_path)
745        if tags is not None:
746            resolved = await resolver.resolve_list("tags", tags)
747            data["tags"] = resolved
748        if not isinstance(archive_serial_number, Unset) and archive_serial_number is not None:
749            data["archive_serial_number"] = archive_serial_number
750        if custom_fields is not None:
751            data["custom_fields"] = json.dumps(custom_fields)
752
753        files = {"document": (file_path.name, file_bytes)}
754        resp = await self._core._session.post("/documents/post_document/", data=data, files=files)
755        task_id: str = resp.text.strip('"')
756        logger.debug("Upload accepted, task_id=%r", task_id)
757
758        if not wait:
759            return task_id
760
761        interval = poll_interval if poll_interval is not None else self._core._poll_interval
762        timeout = poll_timeout if poll_timeout is not None else self._core._poll_timeout
763        return await self._poll_task(task_id, poll_interval=interval, poll_timeout=timeout)

Upload a document to paperless-ngx.

Arguments:
  • file: Path to the file to upload.
  • title: Title to assign to the document.
  • created: Creation date as an ISO-8601 string ("YYYY-MM-DD") or a ~datetime.date object.
  • correspondent: Correspondent to assign, as an ID or name.
  • document_type: Document type to assign, as an ID or name.
  • storage_path: Storage path to assign, as an ID or name.
  • tags: Tags to assign, as IDs or names.
  • archive_serial_number: Archive serial number to assign.
  • custom_fields: List of {"field": <field_id>, "value": ...} dicts.
  • wait: If False (default), returns immediately with the task ID. If True, polls until processing completes.
  • poll_interval: Seconds between task-status checks while waiting for processing to complete (requires wait=True). Overrides the client-level default. When omitted, falls back to the client-level poll_interval (2.0 s unless changed at construction).
  • poll_timeout: Maximum seconds to wait before raising ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError (requires wait=True). Overrides the client-level default. When omitted, falls back to the client-level poll_timeout (60.0 s unless changed at construction).
Returns:

The Celery task ID string when wait=False, or the fully processed ~easypaperless.models.documents.Document when wait=True.

Raises:
  • ~easypaperless.exceptions.UploadError: If processing fails.
  • ~easypaperless.exceptions.TaskTimeoutError: If timeout is exceeded.
async def bulk_add_tag(self, document_ids: List[int], tag: int | str) -> None: 
818    async def bulk_add_tag(self, document_ids: List[int], tag: int | str) -> None:
819        """Add a tag to multiple documents in a single request.
820
821        Args:
822            document_ids: List of document IDs to tag.
823            tag: Tag to add, as an ID or name.
824        """
825        logger.info("Bulk adding tag %r to %d documents", tag, len(document_ids))
826        tag_id = await self._core._resolver.resolve("tags", tag)
827        await self._bulk_edit(document_ids, "add_tag", tag=tag_id)

Add a tag to multiple documents in a single request.

Arguments:
  • document_ids: List of document IDs to tag.
  • tag: Tag to add, as an ID or name.
async def bulk_remove_tag(self, document_ids: List[int], tag: int | str) -> None:

830        """Remove a tag from multiple documents in a single request.
831
832        Args:
833            document_ids: List of document IDs to un-tag.
834            tag: Tag to remove, as an ID or name.
835        """
836        logger.info("Bulk removing tag %r from %d documents", tag, len(document_ids))
837        tag_id = await self._core._resolver.resolve("tags", tag)
838        await self._bulk_edit(document_ids, "remove_tag", tag=tag_id)

Remove a tag from multiple documents in a single request.

Arguments:

  • tag: Tag to remove, as an ID or name.
async def bulk_modify_tags( self, document_ids: List[int], *, add_tags: Optional[List[Union[str, int]]] = None, remove_tags: Optional[List[Union[str, int]]] = None) -> None: 
840    async def bulk_modify_tags(
841        self,
842        document_ids: List[int],
843        *,
844        add_tags: List[int | str] | None = None,
845        remove_tags: List[int | str] | None = None,
846    ) -> None:
847        """Add and/or remove tags on multiple documents atomically.
848
849        Args:
850            document_ids: List of document IDs to modify.
851            add_tags: Tags to add, as IDs or names.
852            remove_tags: Tags to remove, as IDs or names.
853        """
854        logger.info("Bulk modifying tags on %d documents", len(document_ids))
855        resolver = self._core._resolver
856        add_ids = await resolver.resolve_list("tags", add_tags or [])
857        remove_ids = await resolver.resolve_list("tags", remove_tags or [])
858        await self._bulk_edit(document_ids, "modify_tags", add_tags=add_ids, remove_tags=remove_ids)

Add and/or remove tags on multiple documents atomically.

Arguments:
  • document_ids: List of document IDs to modify.
  • add_tags: Tags to add, as IDs or names.
  • remove_tags: Tags to remove, as IDs or names.

async def bulk_set_correspondent(self, document_ids: List[int], correspondent: int | str | None) -> None: 
869    async def bulk_set_correspondent(
870        self, document_ids: List[int], correspondent: int | str | None
871    ) -> None:
872        """Assign a correspondent to multiple documents in a single request.
873

875            document_ids: List of document IDs to modify.

883        if correspondent is not None:

async def bulk_modify_custom_fields( self, document_ids: List[int], *, add_fields: Optional[List[dict[str, Any]]] = None, remove_fields: Optional[List[int]] = None) -> None:

923        document_ids: List[int],
924        *,
925        add_fields: List[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,

929
930        Args:

934        """

Add and/or remove custom field values on multiple documents.

async def bulk_set_permissions( self, document_ids: List[int], *, set_permissions: easypaperless.SetPermissions | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, owner: int | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, merge: bool = False) -> None: 
943    async def bulk_set_permissions(
944        self,
945        document_ids: List[int],
946        *,
947        set_permissions: SetPermissions | Unset = UNSET,
948        owner: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
949        merge: bool = False,
950    ) -> None:
951        """Set permissions and/or owner on multiple documents.
952
953        Args:
954            document_ids: List of document IDs to modify.
955            set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets.
956                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
957            owner: Numeric user ID to assign as document owner.
958                Pass ``None`` to clear the owner.
959                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
960            merge: When ``True``, new permissions are merged with existing ones.
961        """
962        logger.info("Bulk setting permissions on %d documents", len(document_ids))
963        params: dict[str, Any] = {"merge": merge}
964        if not isinstance(set_permissions, Unset):
965            params["set_permissions"] = set_permissions.model_dump()
966        if not isinstance(owner, Unset):
967            params["owner"] = owner
968        await self._bulk_edit(document_ids, "set_permissions", **params)

Set permissions and/or owner on multiple documents.

Arguments:
  • document_ids: List of document IDs to modify.
  • set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets. Omit (or pass ~easypaperless.UNSET) to leave unchanged.
  • owner: Numeric user ID to assign as document owner. Pass None to clear the owner. Omit (or pass ~easypaperless.UNSET) to leave unchanged.
  • merge: When True, new permissions are merged with existing ones.
class NotesResource: 
 43class NotesResource:
 44    """Accessor for document notes: ``client.documents.notes``."""
 45
 46    def __init__(self, core: _ClientCore) -> None:
 47        self._core = core
 48
 49    async def list(
 50        self,
 51        document_id: int,
 52        *,
 53        page: int | None = None,
 54        page_size: int | None = None,
 55    ) -> PagedResult[DocumentNote]:
 56        """Fetch notes attached to a document.
 57
 58        When ``page`` is ``None`` (the default), all pages are fetched
 59        automatically and ``next`` / ``previous`` in the returned
 60        :class:`~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult` are always
 61        ``None``.  When ``page`` is set to a specific integer, only that one
 62        page is fetched and ``next`` / ``previous`` contain the raw API values.
 63
 64        Args:
 65            document_id: Numeric ID of the document whose notes to retrieve.
 66            page: Return only this specific page (1-based).
 67            page_size: Number of results per page.
 68
 69        Returns:
 70            :class:`~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult` of
 71            :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote` objects,
 72            ordered by creation time.
 73
 74        Raises:
 75            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
 76                with that ID.
 77        """
 78        logger.info("Listing notes for document id=%d", document_id)
 79        path = f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/"
 80        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
 81        if page_size is not None:
 82            params["page_size"] = page_size
 83        if page is not None:
 84            params["page"] = page
 85
 86        resp = await self._core._session.get(path, params=params or None)
 87        data = resp.json()
 88
 89        if isinstance(data, list):
 90            # The paperless-ngx notes endpoint returns a plain JSON array rather
 91            # than a paginated envelope.  Build a synthetic PagedResult so callers
 92            # always receive a consistent return type regardless of API version.
 93            notes = [DocumentNote.model_validate(item) for item in data]
 94            note_ids = [n.id for n in notes if n.id is not None]
 95            all_ids: list[int] | None = note_ids if note_ids else None
 96            return PagedResult(
 97                count=len(notes),
 98                next=None,
 99                previous=None,
100                all=all_ids,
101                results=notes,
102            )
103
104        # Paginated dict envelope — forward-compatible with potential future API changes.
105        items: list[Any] = list(data.get("results", []))
106        count: int = data.get("count", 0)
107        page_all_ids: list[int] | None = data.get("all")
108
109        if page is None:
110            next_url: str | None = data.get("next")
111            while next_url:
112                next_url = self._core._session._normalise_next_url(next_url)
113                page_resp = await self._core._session.get(next_url)
114                page_data = page_resp.json()
115                items.extend(page_data.get("results", []))
116                next_url = page_data.get("next")
117            return PagedResult(
118                count=count,
119                next=None,
120                previous=None,
121                all=page_all_ids,
122                results=[DocumentNote.model_validate(item) for item in items],
123            )
124
125        return cast(
126            PagedResult[DocumentNote],
127            PagedResult(
128                count=count,
129                next=data.get("next"),
130                previous=data.get("previous"),
131                all=page_all_ids,
132                results=[DocumentNote.model_validate(item) for item in items],
133            ),
134        )
135
136    async def create(self, document_id: int, *, note: str) -> DocumentNote:
137        """Create a new note on a document.
138
139        Args:
140            document_id: Numeric ID of the document to annotate.
141            note: Text content of the note.
142
143        Returns:
144            The newly created :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote`.
145
146        Raises:
147            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
148                with that ID.
149        """
150        logger.info("Creating note for document id=%d", document_id)
151        resp = await self._core._session.post(
152            f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/",
153            json={"note": note},
154        )
155        data = resp.json()
156        if isinstance(data, list):
157            return DocumentNote.model_validate(data[-1])
158        return DocumentNote.model_validate(data)
159
160    async def delete(self, document_id: int, note_id: int) -> None:
161        """Delete a note from a document.
162
163        Args:
164            document_id: Numeric ID of the document that owns the note.
165            note_id: Numeric ID of the note to delete.
166
167        Raises:
168            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document or note
169                exists with the given IDs.
170        """
171        logger.info("Deleting note id=%d from document id=%d", note_id, document_id)
172        await self._core._session.delete(f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/", params={"id": note_id})

Accessor for document notes: client.documents.notes.

NotesResource(core: easypaperless.client._ClientCore) 
46    def __init__(self, core: _ClientCore) -> None:
47        self._core = core
async def list( self, document_id: int, *, page: int | None = None, page_size: int | None = None) -> easypaperless.PagedResult[DocumentNote]: 
 49    async def list(
 50        self,
 51        document_id: int,
 52        *,
 53        page: int | None = None,
 54        page_size: int | None = None,
 55    ) -> PagedResult[DocumentNote]:
 56        """Fetch notes attached to a document.
 57
 58        When ``page`` is ``None`` (the default), all pages are fetched
 59        automatically and ``next`` / ``previous`` in the returned
 60        :class:`~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult` are always
 61        ``None``.  When ``page`` is set to a specific integer, only that one
 62        page is fetched and ``next`` / ``previous`` contain the raw API values.
 63
 64        Args:
 65            document_id: Numeric ID of the document whose notes to retrieve.
 66            page: Return only this specific page (1-based).
 67            page_size: Number of results per page.
 68
 69        Returns:
 70            :class:`~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult` of
 71            :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote` objects,
 72            ordered by creation time.
 73
 74        Raises:
 75            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
 76                with that ID.
 77        """
 78        logger.info("Listing notes for document id=%d", document_id)
 79        path = f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/"
 80        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
 81        if page_size is not None:
 82            params["page_size"] = page_size
 83        if page is not None:
 84            params["page"] = page
 85
 86        resp = await self._core._session.get(path, params=params or None)
 87        data = resp.json()
 88
 89        if isinstance(data, list):
 90            # The paperless-ngx notes endpoint returns a plain JSON array rather
 91            # than a paginated envelope.  Build a synthetic PagedResult so callers
 92            # always receive a consistent return type regardless of API version.
 93            notes = [DocumentNote.model_validate(item) for item in data]
 94            note_ids = [n.id for n in notes if n.id is not None]
 95            all_ids: list[int] | None = note_ids if note_ids else None
 96            return PagedResult(
 97                count=len(notes),
 98                next=None,
 99                previous=None,
100                all=all_ids,
101                results=notes,
102            )
103
104        # Paginated dict envelope — forward-compatible with potential future API changes.
105        items: list[Any] = list(data.get("results", []))
106        count: int = data.get("count", 0)
107        page_all_ids: list[int] | None = data.get("all")
108
109        if page is None:
110            next_url: str | None = data.get("next")
111            while next_url:
112                next_url = self._core._session._normalise_next_url(next_url)
113                page_resp = await self._core._session.get(next_url)
114                page_data = page_resp.json()
115                items.extend(page_data.get("results", []))
116                next_url = page_data.get("next")
117            return PagedResult(
118                count=count,
119                next=None,
120                previous=None,
121                all=page_all_ids,
122                results=[DocumentNote.model_validate(item) for item in items],
123            )
124
125        return cast(
126            PagedResult[DocumentNote],
127            PagedResult(
128                count=count,
129                next=data.get("next"),
130                previous=data.get("previous"),
131                all=page_all_ids,
132                results=[DocumentNote.model_validate(item) for item in items],
133            ),
134        )

Fetch notes attached to a document.

When page is None (the default), all pages are fetched automatically and next / previous in the returned ~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult are always None. When page is set to a specific integer, only that one page is fetched and next / previous contain the raw API values.

Arguments:
  • document_id: Numeric ID of the document whose notes to retrieve.
  • page: Return only this specific page (1-based).
  • page_size: Number of results per page.
Returns:

~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult of ~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote objects, ordered by creation time.

Raises:
  • ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.
async def create( self, document_id: int, *, note: str) -> easypaperless.DocumentNote: 
136    async def create(self, document_id: int, *, note: str) -> DocumentNote:
137        """Create a new note on a document.
138
139        Args:
140            document_id: Numeric ID of the document to annotate.
141            note: Text content of the note.
142
143        Returns:
144            The newly created :class:`~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote`.
145
146        Raises:
147            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists
148                with that ID.
149        """
150        logger.info("Creating note for document id=%d", document_id)
151        resp = await self._core._session.post(
152            f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/",
153            json={"note": note},
154        )
155        data = resp.json()
156        if isinstance(data, list):
157            return DocumentNote.model_validate(data[-1])
158        return DocumentNote.model_validate(data)

Create a new note on a document.

Arguments:
  • document_id: Numeric ID of the document to annotate.
  • note: Text content of the note.
Returns:

The newly created ~easypaperless.models.documents.DocumentNote.

Raises:
  • ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document exists with that ID.
async def delete(self, document_id: int, note_id: int) -> None: 
160    async def delete(self, document_id: int, note_id: int) -> None:
161        """Delete a note from a document.
162
163        Args:
164            document_id: Numeric ID of the document that owns the note.
165            note_id: Numeric ID of the note to delete.
166
167        Raises:
168            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document or note
169                exists with the given IDs.
170        """
171        logger.info("Deleting note id=%d from document id=%d", note_id, document_id)
172        await self._core._session.delete(f"/documents/{document_id}/notes/", params={"id": note_id})

Delete a note from a document.

Arguments:
  • document_id: Numeric ID of the document that owns the note.
  • note_id: Numeric ID of the note to delete.
Raises:
  • ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no document or note exists with the given IDs.
class StoragePathsResource: 
 21class StoragePathsResource:
 22    """Accessor for storage paths: ``client.storage_paths``."""
 23
 24    def __init__(self, core: _ClientCore) -> None:
 25        self._core = core
 26
 27    async def list(
 28        self,
 29        *,
 30        ids: List[int] | None = None,
 31        name_contains: str | None = None,
 32        name_exact: str | None = None,
 33        path_contains: str | None = None,
 34        path_exact: str | None = None,
 35        page: int | None = None,
 36        page_size: int | None = None,
 37        ordering: str | None = None,
 38        descending: bool = False,
 39    ) -> PagedResult[StoragePath]:
 40        """Return storage paths defined in paperless-ngx.
 41
 42        When ``page`` is ``None`` (the default), all pages are fetched
 43        automatically and ``next`` / ``previous`` in the result are always
 44        ``None``.  When ``page`` is set, only that page is fetched and
 45        ``next`` / ``previous`` reflect the raw API values.
 46
 47        Args:
 48            ids: Return only storage paths whose ID is in this list.
 49            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on name.
 50            name_exact: Case-insensitive exact match on name.
 51            path_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on path template.
 52            path_exact: Case-insensitive exact match on path template.
 53            page: Return only this specific page (1-based).
 54            page_size: Number of results per page.
 55            ordering: Field to sort by.
 56            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction.
 57
 58        Returns:
 59            :class:`~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult` of
 60            :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath` objects.
 61        """
 62        logger.info("Listing storage paths")
 63        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
 64        if ids is not None:
 65            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
 66        if name_contains is not None:
 67            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
 68        if name_exact is not None:
 69            params["name__iexact"] = name_exact
 70        if path_contains is not None:
 71            params["path__icontains"] = path_contains
 72        if path_exact is not None:
 73            params["path__iexact"] = path_exact
 74        if page is not None:
 75            params["page"] = page
 76        if page_size is not None:
 77            params["page_size"] = page_size
 78        if ordering is not None:
 79            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
 80        return cast(
 81            PagedResult[StoragePath],
 82            await self._core._list_resource("storage_paths", StoragePath, params or None),
 83        )
 84
 85    async def get(self, id: int) -> StoragePath:
 86        """Fetch a single storage path by its ID.
 87
 88        Args:
 89            id: Numeric storage-path ID.
 90
 91        Returns:
 92            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath` with the given ID.
 93
 94        Raises:
 95            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path exists with that ID.
 96        """
 97        logger.info("Getting storage path id=%d", id)
 98        return cast(StoragePath, await self._core._get_resource("storage_paths", id, StoragePath))
 99
100    async def create(
101        self,
102        *,
103        name: str,
104        path: str | Unset = UNSET,
105        match: str | Unset = UNSET,
106        matching_algorithm: MatchingAlgorithm | Unset = UNSET,
107        is_insensitive: bool = True,
108        owner: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
109        set_permissions: SetPermissions | None | Unset = UNSET,
110    ) -> StoragePath:
111        """Create a new storage path.
112
113        Args:
114            name: Storage-path name. Must be unique.
115            path: Template string for the archive file path.
116            match: Auto-matching pattern.
117            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied.
118                E.g. ``matching_algorithm=MatchingAlgorithm.REGEX`` for
119                regular-expression matching.
120            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is case-insensitive.
121                Defaults to ``True``, matching the paperless-ngx API default.
122            owner: Numeric user ID to assign as owner.
123            set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets.
124                Pass ``None`` to create with empty permissions.
125
126        Returns:
127            The newly created :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`.
128        """
129        logger.info("Creating storage path name=%r", name)
130        return cast(
131            StoragePath,
132            await self._core._create_resource(
133                "storage_paths",
134                StoragePath,
135                owner=owner,
136                set_permissions=set_permissions,
137                name=name,
138                path=path,
139                match=match,
140                matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
141                is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
142            ),
143        )
144
145    async def update(
146        self,
147        id: int,
148        *,
149        name: str | Unset = UNSET,
150        path: str | Unset = UNSET,
151        match: str | Unset = UNSET,
152        matching_algorithm: MatchingAlgorithm | Unset = UNSET,
153        is_insensitive: bool | Unset = UNSET,
154        owner: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
155        set_permissions: SetPermissions | None | Unset = UNSET,
156    ) -> StoragePath:
157        """Partially update a storage path (PATCH semantics).
158
159        Args:
160            id: Numeric ID of the storage path to update.
161            name: Storage-path name.
162            path: Template string for the archive file path.
163            match: Auto-matching pattern.
164            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied.
165                E.g. ``matching_algorithm=MatchingAlgorithm.REGEX`` for
166                regular-expression matching.
167            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is case-insensitive.
168            owner: Numeric user ID to assign as owner.
169                Pass ``None`` to clear the owner.
170                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
171            set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets.
172                Pass ``None`` to clear all permissions (overwrite with empty).
173                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
174
175        Returns:
176            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.storage_paths.StoragePath`.
177        """
178        logger.info("Updating storage path id=%d", id)
179        return cast(
180            StoragePath,
181            await self._core._update_resource(
182                "storage_paths",
183                id,
184                StoragePath,
185                name=name,
186                path=path,
187                match=match,
188                matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
189                is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
190                owner=owner,
191                set_permissions=set_permissions,
192            ),
193        )
194
195    async def delete(self, id: int) -> None:
196        """Delete a storage path.
197
198        Args:
199            id: Numeric ID of the storage path to delete.
200
201        Raises:
202            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no storage path exists with that ID.
203        """
204        logger.info("Deleting storage path id=%d", id)
205        await self._core._delete_resource("storage_paths", id)
206
207    async def bulk_delete(self, ids: List[int]) -> None:
208        """Permanently delete multiple storage paths in a single request.
209
210        Args:
211            ids: List of storage path IDs to delete.
212        """
213        logger.info("Bulk deleting %d storage paths", len(ids))
214        await self._core._bulk_edit_objects("storage_paths", ids, "delete")
215
216    async def bulk_set_permissions(
217        self,
218        ids: List[int],
219        *,
220        set_permissions: SetPermissions | Unset = UNSET,
221        owner: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
222        merge: bool = False,
223    ) -> None:
224        """Set permissions and/or owner on multiple storage paths.
225
226        Args:
227            ids: List of storage path IDs to modify.
228            set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets.
229                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
230            owner: Numeric user ID to assign as owner.
231                Pass ``None`` to clear the owner.
232                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
233            merge: When ``True``, new permissions are merged with existing ones.
234        """
235        logger.info("Bulk setting permissions on %d storage paths", len(ids))
236        params: dict[str, Any] = {"merge": merge}
237        if not isinstance(set_permissions, Unset):
238            params["permissions"] = set_permissions.model_dump()
239        if not isinstance(owner, Unset):
240            params["owner"] = owner
241        await self._core._bulk_edit_objects("storage_paths", ids, "set_permissions", **params)

Accessor for storage paths: client.storage_paths.

Arguments:
  • ids: List of storage path IDs to delete.
async def bulk_set_permissions( self, ids: List[int], *, set_permissions: easypaperless.SetPermissions | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, owner: int | None | easypaperless.Unset = UNSET, merge: bool = False) -> None: 
216    async def bulk_set_permissions(
217        self,
218        ids: List[int],
219        *,
220        set_permissions: SetPermissions | Unset = UNSET,
221        owner: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
222        merge: bool = False,
223    ) -> None:
224        """Set permissions and/or owner on multiple storage paths.
225
226        Args:
227            ids: List of storage path IDs to modify.
228            set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets.
229                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
230            owner: Numeric user ID to assign as owner.
231                Pass ``None`` to clear the owner.
232                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
233            merge: When ``True``, new permissions are merged with existing ones.
234        """
235        logger.info("Bulk setting permissions on %d storage paths", len(ids))
236        params: dict[str, Any] = {"merge": merge}
237        if not isinstance(set_permissions, Unset):
238            params["permissions"] = set_permissions.model_dump()
239        if not isinstance(owner, Unset):
240            params["owner"] = owner
241        await self._core._bulk_edit_objects("storage_paths", ids, "set_permissions", **params)

Set permissions and/or owner on multiple storage paths.

Arguments:
  • ids: List of storage path IDs to modify.
  • set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets. Omit (or pass ~easypaperless.UNSET) to leave unchanged.
  • owner: Numeric user ID to assign as owner. Pass None to clear the owner. Omit (or pass ~easypaperless.UNSET) to leave unchanged.
  • merge: When True, new permissions are merged with existing ones.
class TagsResource: 
 21class TagsResource:
 22    """Accessor for tags: ``client.tags``."""
 23
 24    def __init__(self, core: _ClientCore) -> None:
 25        self._core = core
 26
 27    async def list(
 28        self,
 29        *,
 30        ids: List[int] | None = None,
 31        name_contains: str | None = None,
 32        name_exact: str | None = None,
 33        page: int | None = None,
 34        page_size: int | None = None,
 35        ordering: str | None = None,
 36        descending: bool = False,
 37    ) -> PagedResult[Tag]:
 38        """Return tags defined in paperless-ngx.
 39
 40        When ``page`` is ``None`` (the default), all pages are fetched
 41        automatically and ``next`` / ``previous`` in the result are always
 42        ``None``.  When ``page`` is set, only that page is fetched and
 43        ``next`` / ``previous`` reflect the raw API values.
 44
 45        Args:
 46            ids: Return only tags whose ID is in this list.
 47            name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on tag name.
 48            name_exact: Case-insensitive exact match on tag name.
 49            page: Return only this specific page (1-based).
 50            page_size: Number of results per page.
 51            ordering: Field to sort by.
 52            descending: When ``True``, reverses the sort direction.
 53
 54        Returns:
 55            :class:`~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult` of
 56            :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag` objects.
 57        """
 58        logger.info("Listing tags")
 59        params: dict[str, Any] = {}
 60        if ids is not None:
 61            params["id__in"] = ",".join(str(i) for i in ids)
 62        if name_contains is not None:
 63            params["name__icontains"] = name_contains
 64        if name_exact is not None:
 65            params["name__iexact"] = name_exact
 66        if page is not None:
 67            params["page"] = page
 68        if page_size is not None:
 69            params["page_size"] = page_size
 70        if ordering is not None:
 71            params["ordering"] = f"-{ordering}" if descending else ordering
 72        return cast(
 73            PagedResult[Tag],
 74            await self._core._list_resource("tags", Tag, params or None),
 75        )
 76
 77    async def get(self, id: int) -> Tag:
 78        """Fetch a single tag by its ID.
 79
 80        Args:
 81            id: Numeric tag ID.
 82
 83        Returns:
 84            The :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag` with the given ID.
 85
 86        Raises:
 87            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with
 88                that ID.
 89        """
 90        logger.info("Getting tag id=%d", id)
 91        return cast(Tag, await self._core._get_resource("tags", id, Tag))
 92
 93    async def create(
 94        self,
 95        *,
 96        name: str,
 97        color: str | Unset = UNSET,
 98        is_inbox_tag: bool | Unset = UNSET,
 99        match: str | Unset = UNSET,
100        matching_algorithm: MatchingAlgorithm | Unset = UNSET,
101        is_insensitive: bool = True,
102        parent: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
103        owner: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
104        set_permissions: SetPermissions | None | Unset = UNSET,
105    ) -> Tag:
106        """Create a new tag.
107
108        Args:
109            name: Tag name. Must be unique.
110            color: Background colour as a CSS hex string.
111            is_inbox_tag: When ``True``, newly ingested documents get this tag.
112            match: Auto-matching pattern.
113            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied.
114                E.g. ``matching_algorithm=MatchingAlgorithm.REGEX`` for
115                regular-expression matching.
116            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is case-insensitive.
117                Defaults to ``True``, matching the paperless-ngx API default.
118            parent: ID of parent tag for hierarchical trees.
119            owner: Numeric user ID to assign as owner.
120            set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets.
121                Pass ``None`` to create with empty permissions.
122
123        Returns:
124            The newly created :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag`.
125        """
126        logger.info("Creating tag name=%r", name)
127        return cast(
128            Tag,
129            await self._core._create_resource(
130                "tags",
131                Tag,
132                owner=owner,
133                set_permissions=set_permissions,
134                name=name,
135                color=color,
136                is_inbox_tag=is_inbox_tag,
137                match=match,
138                matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
139                is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
140                parent=parent,
141            ),
142        )
143
144    async def update(
145        self,
146        id: int,
147        *,
148        name: str | Unset = UNSET,
149        color: str | Unset = UNSET,
150        is_inbox_tag: bool | Unset = UNSET,
151        match: str | Unset = UNSET,
152        matching_algorithm: MatchingAlgorithm | Unset = UNSET,
153        is_insensitive: bool | Unset = UNSET,
154        parent: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
155        owner: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
156        set_permissions: SetPermissions | None | Unset = UNSET,
157    ) -> Tag:
158        """Partially update a tag (PATCH semantics).
159
160        Args:
161            id: Numeric ID of the tag to update.
162            name: Tag name.
163            color: Background colour as a CSS hex string.
164            is_inbox_tag: When ``True``, newly ingested documents get this tag.
165            match: Auto-matching pattern.
166            matching_algorithm: Controls how ``match`` is applied.
167                E.g. ``matching_algorithm=MatchingAlgorithm.REGEX`` for
168                regular-expression matching.
169            is_insensitive: When ``True``, ``match`` is case-insensitive.
170            parent: ID of parent tag.
171                Pass ``None`` to clear (make root tag).
172                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
173            owner: Numeric user ID to assign as owner.
174                Pass ``None`` to clear the owner.
175                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
176            set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets.
177                Pass ``None`` to clear all permissions (overwrite with empty).
178                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
179
180        Returns:
181            The updated :class:`~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag`.
182        """
183        logger.info("Updating tag id=%d", id)
184        return cast(
185            Tag,
186            await self._core._update_resource(
187                "tags",
188                id,
189                Tag,
190                name=name,
191                color=color,
192                is_inbox_tag=is_inbox_tag,
193                match=match,
194                matching_algorithm=matching_algorithm,
195                is_insensitive=is_insensitive,
196                parent=parent,
197                owner=owner,
198                set_permissions=set_permissions,
199            ),
200        )
201
202    async def delete(self, id: int) -> None:
203        """Delete a tag.
204
205        Args:
206            id: Numeric ID of the tag to delete.
207
208        Raises:
209            ~easypaperless.exceptions.NotFoundError: If no tag exists with
210                that ID.
211        """
212        logger.info("Deleting tag id=%d", id)
213        await self._core._delete_resource("tags", id)
214
215    async def bulk_delete(self, ids: List[int]) -> None:
216        """Permanently delete multiple tags in a single request.
217
218        Args:
219            ids: List of tag IDs to delete.
220        """
221        logger.info("Bulk deleting %d tags", len(ids))
222        await self._core._bulk_edit_objects("tags", ids, "delete")
223
224    async def bulk_set_permissions(
225        self,
226        ids: List[int],
227        *,
228        set_permissions: SetPermissions | Unset = UNSET,
229        owner: int | None | Unset = UNSET,
230        merge: bool = False,
231    ) -> None:
232        """Set permissions and/or owner on multiple tags.
233
234        Args:
235            ids: List of tag IDs to modify.
236            set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets.
237                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
238            owner: Numeric user ID to assign as owner.
239                Pass ``None`` to clear the owner.
240                Omit (or pass :data:`~easypaperless.UNSET`) to leave unchanged.
241            merge: When ``True``, new permissions are merged with existing ones.
242        """
243        logger.info("Bulk setting permissions on %d tags", len(ids))
244        params: dict[str, Any] = {"merge": merge}
245        if not isinstance(set_permissions, Unset):
246            params["permissions"] = set_permissions.model_dump()
247        if not isinstance(owner, Unset):
248            params["owner"] = owner
249        await self._core._bulk_edit_objects("tags", ids, "set_permissions", **params)

Return tags defined in paperless-ngx.

When page is None (the default), all pages are fetched automatically and next / previous in the result are always None. When page is set, only that page is fetched and next / previous reflect the raw API values.

Arguments:
  • ids: Return only tags whose ID is in this list.
  • name_contains: Case-insensitive substring filter on tag name.
  • name_exact: Case-insensitive exact match on tag name.
  • page: Return only this specific page (1-based).
  • page_size: Number of results per page.
  • ordering: Field to sort by.
  • descending: When True, reverses the sort direction.
Returns:

~easypaperless.models.paged_result.PagedResult of ~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag objects.

Create a new tag.

Arguments:
  • name: Tag name. Must be unique.
  • color: Background colour as a CSS hex string.
  • is_inbox_tag: When True, newly ingested documents get this tag.
  • match: Auto-matching pattern.
  • matching_algorithm: Controls how match is applied. E.g. matching_algorithm=MatchingAlgorithm.REGEX for regular-expression matching.
  • is_insensitive: When True, match is case-insensitive. Defaults to True, matching the paperless-ngx API default.
  • parent: ID of parent tag for hierarchical trees.
  • owner: Numeric user ID to assign as owner.
  • set_permissions: Explicit view/change permission sets. Pass None to create with empty permissions.
Returns:

The newly created ~easypaperless.models.tags.Tag.
